title: Fix R1336mzz(Z) catch test failures: stale hs_anchor h/s and source_eos_hash
description: The recent commit a3a80403 (set IdealGasHelmholtzLead a1/a2 to match REFPROP PX0) shifted the absolute h and s scale for R1336mzz(Z), but the JSON-stored hs_anchor.hmolar/smolar and SUPERANCILLARY.source_eos_hash values were not updated. CI test_catch2.yml fails on master with: (1) Ancillaries.cpp:383-384 hs_anchor h,s mismatch, (2) CoolProp-Tests.cpp:2387-2388 'Check state for hs_anchor for R1336mzz(Z) for reference state DEF', (3) CoolProp-Tests.cpp:3676 source_eos_hash mismatch. EOS now computes hmolar=86080.94, smolar=301.62 (vs stored 31805.05, 113.60). New EOS hash is 99a5b7a9c650456b (vs stored e3a31969b69c06c1).
close_reason: Fixed in PR #2784 — refreshed hs_anchor h/s and re-injected SUPERANCILLARY (new hash 3b8b3cc73b1d9418) via fitcheb. Full catch2 suite green locally (135/125/10/0).

title: Tier 1.1 — Switch dev_clangformat.yml to PR-only trigger
description:
dev_clangformat.yml currently runs on push to master, so master keeps going red after every merge that introduces a violation (latest example: src/Tests/CoolProp-Tests.cpp after commit 8ea3624). Combined with branch protection (out of scope), PR-only triggering is what actually stops violations from landing.

Action:
- Edit .github/workflows/dev_clangformat.yml: remove the push trigger entirely (or restrict to non-master branches like dev_checks)
- Verify the existing dev/ci/clang-format.sh logic still works for PR-only mode (it already takes BASE_SHA + HEAD_SHA)

Acceptance: workflow only runs on pull_request events; master CI no longer goes red on retroactive violations.

title: GUI deployment: Windows code signing (SignPath OSS or Azure Trusted Signing)
description: Apply for SignPath.io OSS sponsorship (free for qualifying OSS) OR set up Azure Trusted Signing (pay-per-signature, no HSM). Wire the chosen mechanism into gui_builder.yml as a post-bundle step that signs both the bundled .exe and the produced .msi/.exe NSIS installer. Update the README's Code signing section to document the path actually taken.
acceptance_criteria: SmartScreen no longer flags freshly downloaded installers; signed binaries verify with Get-AuthenticodeSignature.
notes: CI wiring done in 163dc43f — gui_builder.yml has a post-build 'Sign Windows .exe and .msi' step that activates when WINDOWS_CERTIFICATE + WINDOWS_CERTIFICATE_PASSWORD secrets are added; signtool path supports any Authenticode cert. SignPath OSS path documented in README as the recommended free OSS option (separate action). Awaiting cert/SignPath account by maintainer.

title: GUI deployment: macOS Developer ID + notarization secrets
description: Acquire an Apple Developer ID Application certificate ($99/yr), generate a notarytool API key (.p8), and add the GitHub repo secrets the workflow already plumbs: APPLE_CERTIFICATE (base64 of the .p12), APPLE_CERTIFICATE_PASSWORD, APPLE_SIGNING_IDENTITY, APPLE_TEAM_ID, plus either APPLE_ID + APPLE_PASSWORD (app-specific) or APPLE_API_ISSUER + APPLE_API_KEY + APPLE_API_KEY_PATH (API-key flow). gui_builder.yml's Configure macOS signing step activates automatically once APPLE_CERTIFICATE is set.
acceptance_criteria: Signed and notarized .dmg from a tagged release launches on a fresh macOS without the xattr -dr com.apple.quarantine workaround.
notes: CI wiring + setup docs done in 163dc43f (gui_builder.yml Configure macOS signing step + README acquisition walkthrough). Awaiting Apple Developer ID + secret addition by maintainer to activate.

title: Fix R-1336mzz(Z) enthalpy/entropy reference state mismatch with REFPROP
description:
CI test failure in 'Enthalpy and entropy reference state' check (REFPROPMixtureBackend.cpp:2338-2339) for R-1336mzz(Z), introduced in PR #2781 (NIST IR 8570 mixture pairs).

Failing assertions:
- CHECK( std::abs(DH / h_RP) < 0.01 ) — got 0.901
- CHECK( std::abs(DS / s_RP) < 0.01 ) — got 0.766

Values:
- h_CP := 36244.058,  h_RP := 367081.535
- s_CP := 349.438,    s_RP := 1495.521
- delta_a1 := -22.6138265880399167
- delta_a2 := 14.6859102235818870
- RPName: R1336MZZZ, RRP=8.31445980, RCP=8.314462618

The CoolProp ideal-gas Helmholtz spec (IdealGasHelmholtzCP0PolyT with c=4.0 plus two PlanckEinstein terms at v=736, 2299) is producing h/s that disagree with REFPROP's R1336MZZZ at the reference state by an order of magnitude. Likely a reference-state offset (a1/a2) or cp0 coefficient discrepancy with the REFPROP FLD file.

Run: https://github.com/CoolProp/CoolProp/actions/runs/24936263809/job/73022241015
close_reason: Fixed in fe4f40f4: set Lead a1=-22.6138365893630073, a2=14.6859157463353789 derived from REFPROP PX0 (R1336MZZZ) minus CP0PolyT integration constants. Local 'Check REFPROP and CoolProp values agree' suite passes (1016 assertions); existing [R1336mzzZ] and [NIST-IR-8570] tests still green.

title: Tier 4.1 — One-shot whole-repo clang-format pass + .git-blame-ignore-revs
description:
Apply clang-format to every committed C/C++ file under src/ and include/ in a single PR. Add the merge SHA to .git-blame-ignore-revs so git blame skips it. Document the local config: git config blame.ignoreRevsFile .git-blame-ignore-revs.

Why now: enabling stricter diff-based gates (Tier 2) is cleaner if the legacy formatting backlog is gone — otherwise PR diffs constantly tangle with formatting churn near touched lines. Must land after Tier 1.1 so the master push CI does not re-light over the noisy commit.

Risk: noisy commit. Mitigations: separate PR with no functional changes, an explicit blame-ignore-revs entry, no semantic diffs.

Acceptance:
- After the reformat lands, dev/ci/clang-format.sh HEAD master produces no diff
- .git-blame-ignore-revs file exists at repo root and contains the reformat SHA
- dev/ci/README documents the blame-ignore-revs config
- Test suite passes unchanged on the reformat commit

title: Tier 2.3 — Generate compile_commands.json from CMake
description:
clang-tidy (Tier 2.1, 2.2) and IWYU (Tier 3.4) both need compile_commands.json. CMake supports CMAKE_EXPORT_COMPILE_COMMANDS=ON; verify it produces a usable JSON for the standard CoolProp build options used in CI.

Action:
- Verify cmake -DCMAKE_EXPORT_COMPILE_COMMANDS=ON -DCOOLPROP_MY_MAIN=dev/ci/main.cpp -B build -S . produces compile_commands.json with no unresolved-include errors when fed to clang-tidy
- If header paths are missing for src/ files, augment per-target include directives in CMakeLists.txt
- Document the build command in dev/ci/README

Acceptance: clang-tidy --warnings-as-errors=* (using the .clang-tidy config) runs cleanly against a sample src/ file using only the generated compile_commands.json — no module/include surprises.

title: Tier 2.1 — New workflow: clang-tidy-diff on PR-touched lines
description:
clang-tidy-diff.py (ships with LLVM) runs clang-tidy only on lines changed in a unified diff. This avoids drowning PR authors in the legacy 937KLOC warning backlog while still enforcing the existing strict .clang-tidy config (WarningsAsErrors: *) on new code.

Action:
- New .github/workflows/dev_clangtidy.yml; pull_request trigger only (initially)
- Build with -DCMAKE_EXPORT_COMPILE_COMMANDS=ON (depends on Tier 2.3)
- git diff origin/<base>..HEAD -U0 | clang-tidy-diff.py -p1 -path build (inherits checks + WarningsAsErrors from .clang-tidy)
- Fail the job if any warning surfaces on changed lines

Acceptance:
- A contrived PR with a deliberate violation on changed lines fails
- A PR that does not touch a flagged-but-legacy file passes
- Runs in <10 min on a typical PR (lines, not whole project)

title: Tier 1.4 — Document local code-quality workflow
description:
One page so a new contributor can satisfy every gate without spelunking. Likely home: dev/ci/README.md (new) or extend CONTRIBUTING.md.

Cover:
- How to run clang-format locally: cmake --build build --target format, dev/ci/clang-format.sh against branch, or pre-commit
- Pre-commit setup (pip install pre-commit && pre-commit install)
- How to run clang-tidy diff-locally on touched files (after Tier 2 lands)
- The .git-blame-ignore-revs setup: git config blame.ignoreRevsFile .git-blame-ignore-revs (after Tier 4 lands)

Acceptance: a new contributor can read one page and run all the local checks; doc is referenced from README.

title: Tier 1.3 — Add .pre-commit-config.yaml with clang-format hook
description:
Catch clang-format violations before contributors push. Use the standard pre-commit framework with the upstream mirrors-clang-format hook.

Action:
- Add .pre-commit-config.yaml with the clang-format-hook pinned to clang-format-18 (matches CI image — Ubuntu clang-format 18.1.3)
- Limit to changed C/C++ files (extensions c, cpp, h, hpp); exclude externals/ and vendored code
- Add a pre-commit installation note in the dev workflow doc (Tier 1.4)

Acceptance: contributors who pip install pre-commit && pre-commit install get clang-format auto-checks on staged C/C++ files.

title: Tier 1.2 — Add CMake format target for repo-wide clang-format
description:
Give contributors a one-liner to format the codebase: cmake --build build --target format. Mirror the find pattern from dev/ci/clang-format.sh so behavior matches CI.

Action:
- In CMakeLists.txt: find_program(CLANG_FORMAT clang-format), add custom_target(format ...) that runs clang-format -i over src/ and include/ (extensions: c, cpp, h, hpp)
- Skip externals/ and any vendored third-party
- Pin or document the expected clang-format version (CI uses 18.x)

Acceptance: cmake --build build --target format formats every committed C/C++ source under src/ and include/ and matches dev/ci/clang-format.sh output exactly.

title: [epic] C++ code quality gates: clang-format, clang-tidy, cppcheck, CodeQL, one-shot reformat
description:
Lightweight code-quality program for the C++ codebase, driven by the observation that clang-format CI has been failing on master and getting ignored. Goal: gates that are actually satisfiable so they stop being bypassed.

Approach — four tiers, all to be delivered:
- Tier 1 — Stop the bleeding: green up clang-format, add local hooks/targets, switch CI to PR-only triggers.
- Tier 2 — clang-tidy on PR-diff only (not the 937KLOC backlog).
- Tier 3 — Reactivate dormant tools (cppcheck, CodeQL, Coverity, IWYU) with PR-friendly triggers; warning-only where appropriate.
- Tier 4 — One-shot whole-repo clang-format pass + safe clang-tidy --fix, with .git-blame-ignore-revs.

Design constraint: diff-only enforcement. Do not gate on legacy 937KLOC code; only flag new violations. Children carry the per-task detail.

title: GUI deployment: bundle third-party license notices in an About dialog
description: Wire `cargo about` (Rust deps) + `license-checker` (npm deps) into the build to emit an aggregated NOTICE/LICENSES file bundled into the .app/.exe/.deb. Add a minimal About dialog exposing it, plus the CoolProp version (git describe) and the Tauri/React versions. Required by MIT/Apache-2.0 dep license terms.
acceptance_criteria: About dialog reachable from the menu bar, displays CoolProp + Tauri + React versions and a scrollable third-party notices view.
close_reason: Completed in 67b9cf44 — license aggregation script (cargo metadata + license-checker) generates src/generated/notices.ts at build time (~474 Rust + ~273 npm packages, ~68 KB). About modal in App header surfaces the version + collapsible notices view. Wired via prebuild/predev/pretest hooks.

title: GUI deployment: release tagging policy + automated GH release publishing
description: Decide a tag scheme (e.g. `gui-v0.1.0` to disambiguate from CoolProp library tags). Configure tauri-action in gui_builder.yml to publish a draft GitHub Release on `gui-v*` tags with all platform bundles attached. Document the release dance (bump version in package.json + tauri.conf.json + Cargo.toml, tag, push, edit + publish the draft) in wrappers/GUI/README.md.
acceptance_criteria: Pushing a gui-v0.1.0 tag yields a draft release with .dmg + .msi + .deb + .AppImage + .rpm attached, ready to publish.
close_reason: Completed in 163dc43f — workflow tag trigger narrowed to gui-v* and tauri-action wired with conditional tagName/releaseName so a draft Release is created on gui-v* tag pushes. Release dance documented in README.

title: GUI deployment: Tauri auto-updater with signed manifests
description: Enable the @tauri-apps/plugin-updater, generate a Tauri updater signing keypair (separate from the OS signing certs), and on tag push publish a signed update manifest (latest.json) + per-platform bundles to GitHub Releases. Wire an update-check call on app start with a user-visible 'restart to update' prompt. Document the updater key custody (likely a repo secret + offline backup).
acceptance_criteria: Running an older bundle finds the new release, verifies its signature, downloads, and on next launch is the new version.
notes: CI wiring + frontend UI + plugin registration done in deefe7f6. Awaiting maintainer to run the keypair ceremony documented in README (npx tauri signer generate, embed pubkey, add TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY + TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY_PASSWORD secrets, back up private key offline).

title: GUI testing: macOS launch + screenshot in CI
description: Extend gui_builder.yml's smoke-test step to macos-latest (currently only Linux + Windows have a launch+screenshot test). Use `screencapture -x` to grab the desktop after launching the bundled .app, verify the binary stays alive past 8s, upload macos-startup.png alongside the existing linux-startup.png and windows-startup.png artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: macOS CI job captures and uploads macos-startup.png; failure path catches launch crashes.
close_reason: Completed in 163dc43f — macOS launch+screenshot smoke test added, screencapture -x captures macos-startup.png alongside linux/windows screenshots.

title: GUI testing: end-to-end smoke flow via tauri-driver
description: Wire tauri-driver + WebdriverIO so we can exercise the actual app end-to-end: launch the binary, open the Property Calculator, compute Water at PT_INPUTS=(101325,300) and assert the resulting Dmass is within tolerance of 996 kg/m³; build a saturation table for R134a; switch to Humid Air, calculate at T=293.15 R=0.5, verify isolines render. Run on ubuntu-22.04 under Xvfb in CI.
acceptance_criteria: tauri-driver e2e test passes in CI; on failure, the test produces a screenshot + log uploaded as artifact.

title: GUI testing: Rust backend unit tests (state_manager, FFI)
description: Add #[cfg(test)] modules to wrappers/GUI/src-tauri/src/state_manager.rs and coolprop_ffi.rs covering: handle-table create/update/get/free lifecycle, error propagation when CoolProp returns a non-empty errstring, compute_saturation_table edge cases (n_points<2, empty params list, sweep boundaries), and HAPropsSI input validation via the probe-output trick.
acceptance_criteria: cargo test in src-tauri passes; tests run as a CI step on all three OSes.
close_reason: Completed in 95d8a4cd — 9 Rust tests covering FFI shims (FluidsList, Props1SI, create/update/free lifecycle, error paths, HAPropsSI) and state-manager commands (handle table, fluid limits, saturation table, humid air). Wired into CI via cargo test --release on ubuntu-22.04.

title: GUI testing: frontend unit tests (Vitest + RTL)
description: Add Vitest + React Testing Library, write unit tests for components with non-trivial logic: PropertyCalculator (input-pair switching, basis-flip resets, compute error path), SaturationTable (column switching by basis, copy-TSV correctness), SaturationView (multi-panel add/close/state freeing), HumidAirCalculator (isolines toggle + clip at sat curve), and the hooks (useDraggableSplit clamp, useAbstractState lifecycle and free-on-unmount). Wire `npm run test` into gui_builder.yml as a pre-build step on ubuntu-22.04.
acceptance_criteria: npm run test passes locally and in CI; coverage on the listed components/hooks; failure surfaces as a CI red.
close_reason: Completed in fe163896 — Vitest + RTL set up, 16 tests passing (6 useDraggableSplit, 5 useAbstractState, 5 PropertyCalculator). Wired into CI via npm test on ubuntu-22.04.

title: EPIC: CoolProp Desktop GUI (Tauri 2)
description:
Native cross-platform desktop GUI for CoolProp under wrappers/GUI/, built with Tauri 2 (Rust + native WebView), React/TypeScript/Vite, and Plotly. CoolProp is statically linked. Branch: ib/GUI.

Tabs implemented: Property Calculator (HEOS-only, 12 input pairs, draggable splitter), multi-panel Saturation Tables, Humid Air calculator with optional isolines through the computed state point.

CI (.github/workflows/gui_builder.yml) builds bundles on macOS / Windows / Ubuntu 22.04, runs post-build smoke tests (.deb on ubuntu:26.04 forward-compat, .msi silent install + launch), and captures startup screenshots on Linux+Windows.

Open work falls into two areas tracked by the children of this epic:
  - Testing: unit, e2e, visual-regression, performance.
  - Deployment: code signing (macOS + Windows), auto-update, package-manager submissions, release process.

Currently shipping unsigned bundles. macOS quarantine workaround documented in wrappers/GUI/README.md.

title: Assessment: decision memo (which method ships)
description: Synthesize benchmark + robustness results into a recommendation: ship Gernert, ship Chebyshev, ship both behind a switch, or keep production as-is. Include rollout plan and any flags/options needed.
acceptance_criteria: Memo committed to dev/ as a markdown file; key non-obvious findings saved via bd remember.

title: Assessment: robustness test set
description: Curated set of (T,p,z) points known to be hard: near-critical loci, three-phase-adjacent compositions, very-low-T heavy hydrocarbons, very-high-p, metastable wells. Each method runs against this set and is scored on convergence rate and accuracy vs REFPROP/known answer.
acceptance_criteria: Test set lives in dev/density_rootfinding_hardpoints.csv; harness consumes it and emits a per-method scorecard.

title: Assessment: unified benchmark harness
description: Extend dev/bench_ch4_h2s_grid.cpp into a method-agnostic harness that runs all three solvers (production, Gernert, Chebyshev) over the same (T,p,z) grid, emitting CSV with method, timing, iterations, residual, and result density. Reuse existing CSV format where possible.
acceptance_criteria: One CSV per fluid system, one binary that takes --method=production|gernert|chebyshev.

title: Chebyshev: test suite
description: Mirror the Gernert test suite structure for solver_rho_Tp_Chebyshev. Particular attention to behavior at the rectangle boundaries (must error rather than extrapolate, per design constraint).

title: Chebyshev: implement mixture density rootfinder
description: Implement the Chebyshev-based mixture density rootfinder per spec. Reuse the pure-fluid Chebyshev infrastructure from the superanc work where possible (worktree-cheb has prior art). Entry point: solver_rho_Tp_Chebyshev, parallel to solver_rho_Tp_Gernert.
acceptance_criteria: Builds cleanly; smoke test on AGA-8 grid; behind a flag.

title: Chebyshev: spec mixture extension
description: Pure-fluid Chebyshev superancillary uses τ-only (or τ,δ on a rectangle) expansions. For mixtures, composition z is a new degree of freedom and a hard rectangle in (τ,δ) is composition-dependent (reducing functions). Three candidate strategies to evaluate before coding: (1) build the Chebyshev expansion at runtime per composition (slow setup, fast solve); (2) interpolate a tensor of Chebyshev coefficients in z (memory-heavy, fast setup); (3) hybrid — build only when a (T,p) call cannot be served by current expansion. Pick one, justify, write spec.
design: Constraint from memory: hard-rectangle in (τ,δ), no extrapolation; δ-compactification was tried and rejected (Jacobian blow-up). Target n=8–12 per piece, single-digit μs.
acceptance_criteria: Chosen strategy + rationale + pseudocode in --notes.
notes: Architecture (corrected): per-term Chebyshev expansion in delta ONLY. Each generalized-exponential term factors as kappa(tau)*phi(delta); precompute phi(delta) once per term per EOS load (or ship as JSON). At runtime: evaluate kappa(tau) closed-form, scale phi coefs, sum across terms with corresponding-states weights to get mixture P(delta) Chebyshev for free. Rootfind in delta on that polynomial. ZERO build cost at runtime, ZERO composition dependence in precomputation. Memory ~50 KB/fluid + ~30 KB/binary, ~1.8 MB for 10-comp Amarillo. Non-separable terms (NonAnalytic, SAFTAssociating, GenCubic) handled by direct-eval + Chebyshev sample at the rectangle nodes. Full spec in dev/cheb_mixture_spec.md.

title: Gernert: test suite
description: Tests for solver_rho_Tp_Gernert covering: pure fluid (degenerates correctly), simple binary, AGA-8 mixture, near-critical, and known-hard low-T case. Cross-check density against solver_rho_Tp where the latter converges, and against REFPROP where available.
acceptance_criteria: Test cases live in src/Tests/ following the mixture-pair test pattern (see memory).

title: Gernert: implement density rootfinder
description: Implement the Gernert density-rootfinding scheme as a new entry point alongside solver_rho_Tp. Do NOT replace the production path yet — keep both available behind a flag for benchmarking. Suggested location: src/Backends/Helmholtz/HelmholtzEOSMixtureBackend.cpp, named solver_rho_Tp_Gernert.
acceptance_criteria: Builds cleanly; passes a smoke test on the AGA-8 Amarillo grid; behind a build/runtime flag so production path is undisturbed.

title: Gernert: spec the density-rootfinding algorithm
description: Gernert (2014) PhD thesis, TU Bochum. The thesis is already cited in VLERoutines.cpp for stability analysis but NOT for the density-rootfinding inner loop. Read §3 of the thesis (and the 2014 J. Chem. Thermo. paper if relevant), extract the algorithm: bracketing strategy, Newton/safeguarded steps, termination criteria, special-case handling near the spinodal. Produce a pseudocode spec in the issue notes.
design: Output is a spec, not code. The spec drives the implementation issue.
acceptance_criteria: Pseudocode + cited equation numbers + list of edge cases in --notes.

title: Baseline: characterize solver_rho_Tp failure modes
description: Before adding new solvers, document where the current path breaks. Run dev/bench_ch4_h2s_grid.cpp pattern across: (1) Amarillo natural gas grid (already wired), (2) CH4/H2S, (3) a near-critical CO2/N2 case, (4) low-T heavy mix. Record per (T,p,z): wallclock, iteration count, which fallback tier resolved it, and any failure. Output CSV + a short markdown summary.
acceptance_criteria: CSV of grid results checked into dev/; one-paragraph failure-mode summary saved via bd remember.
close_reason: Baseline characterized across 4 systems (amarillo/ch4h2s/co2n2/c10c1, 60x60 PT grids). CSV+PNG artifacts in dev/, summary in dev/bench_density_BASELINE.md. Key finding: solver_rho_Tp(rho_guess=-1) fails on 25-65% of cells where the full flash succeeds, clustered along phase boundaries. Findings saved via bd remember. Tier instrumentation gap filed as CoolProp-29n.

title: EPIC: Mixture density rootfinding — Gernert vs Chebyshev assessment
description: Production solver is HelmholtzEOSMixtureBackend::solver_rho_Tp (SRK guess + Newton, with solver_rho_Tp_global fallback). It fails or is slow in: near-critical regions, low-T heavy-hydrocarbon mixtures, and metastable branches inside the phase envelope. We have two candidate replacements: (A) Gernert's density-rootfinding scheme (Gernert PhD thesis 2014, TU Bochum), and (B) Chebyshev rootfinding extended from the pure-fluid superancillary work. Goal: implement both, benchmark on a common grid, and decide which (if either) replaces the production path.
design: Hard requirement: any replacement must be at least as robust as solver_rho_Tp_global on the existing test suite. Stretch: single-digit μs per evaluation in the bulk of the (T,p,z) domain.

title: Tier 4.2 — Apply safe clang-tidy --fix passes
description:
After Tier 4.1 lands, apply auto-fixable clang-tidy checks of low semantic risk in follow-up commits. Each fix family in its own commit so any regression bisects cleanly. Each SHA appended to .git-blame-ignore-revs.

Initial scope (review dry-run before each):
- modernize-use-nullptr
- modernize-use-override (with existing AllowOverrideAndFinal=true, IgnoreDestructors=true)
- readability-braces-around-statements
- readability-redundant-string-init
- modernize-use-using

OUT of scope (semantic risk): cppcoreguidelines-* (most), narrowing-conversions, performance-* that change call patterns, anything modifying numeric types.

Workflow per check family:
1. run-clang-tidy -checks=<single check> -fix on a clean tree
2. cmake --build build --target format (re-format after tidy edits)
3. Run full Catch test suite
4. git commit; append SHA to .git-blame-ignore-revs in the same commit

Acceptance: each commit independently passes the existing test suite; SHAs added to .git-blame-ignore-revs; no semantic changes.

title: Tier 3.2 — Reroute CodeQL to run on PRs and master
description:
.github/workflows/dev_codeql.yml currently runs only on the dev_checks branch + 2x weekly schedule, so it is effectively off for active development. With queries: +security-and-quality on cpp + python, this is meaningful coverage that should run on real PRs.

Action:
- Add pull_request trigger for branches: [master]
- Switch push trigger from dev_checks to master (keep schedule as fallback)
- Update github/codeql-action/init@v2 → v3, autobuild@v2 → v3, analyze@v2 → v3
- Update actions/checkout@v3 → v4

Acceptance: CodeQL analysis runs on every PR; results appear in the repo Security tab.

title: Tier 3.1 — Re-enable cppcheck workflow as warning-only
description:
.github/disabled_workflows/dev_cppcheck.yml exists and is partially functional but uses an outdated container (nrel/cppcheck:2.3 from 2020) and deprecated actions. Re-activate as a warning-only artifact step — never fail PRs.

Action:
- Move dev_cppcheck.yml from disabled_workflows/ to .github/workflows/
- Replace nrel/cppcheck:2.3 image with apt-installed cppcheck on ubuntu-latest (current Ubuntu cppcheck is ~2.13)
- Update actions/checkout@v3 → v4 and actions/upload-artifact@v3 → v7 (matches the rest of the repo)
- Force exit 0 in the cppcheck step (continue-on-error or trailing || true) so it never blocks PRs
- Keep the colorize_cppcheck_results.py post-processing for the artifact

Acceptance: PR runs surface a cppcheck artifact + summary annotation but never block the PR. Artifact contains the colorized text.

title: Tier 2.2 — Pre-commit hook for clang-tidy
description:
Extend .pre-commit-config.yaml (Tier 1.3) to also run clang-tidy on changed C/C++ files so contributors get the same diff-only feedback locally that CI gives them. Requires compile_commands.json (Tier 2.3).

Action:
- Add a clang-tidy hook to .pre-commit-config.yaml; configure it to invoke clang-tidy with -p build (or wherever compile_commands.json is)
- Document a one-time CMake config step so the hook can find compile_commands.json
- Make the hook skippable for contributors who do not have a build dir (warn, do not fail)

Acceptance: a contrived violation on a staged file fails pre-commit locally with the same message CI emits.

title: GUI deployment: Flathub manifest + appstream metadata
description: Author a Flathub YAML manifest (org.coolprop.gui.yml) and an AppStream metainfo XML, build against the GNOME or Freedesktop runtime, submit to flathub/flathub. Provides sandboxed Linux distribution decoupled from distro versions. Handles the webkit2gtk runtime via the Flatpak runtime instead of declaring deb/rpm deps.
acceptance_criteria: flatpak install flathub org.coolprop.gui succeeds on a fresh Fedora and a fresh Debian; updates via flatpak update.

title: GUI deployment: winget package manifest
description: Once signed Windows MSIs publish to GitHub Releases, author and submit a winget manifest to microsoft/winget-pkgs (CoolProp.CoolProp.yaml + InstallerType: msi). Goal: `winget install CoolProp` works out of the box; updates via `winget upgrade`.
acceptance_criteria: winget install CoolProp succeeds on a clean Windows 11; winget upgrade installs subsequent releases.

title: GUI deployment: Homebrew Cask formula
description: Once signed/notarized macOS bundles publish to GitHub Releases, submit a Cask formula to homebrew/homebrew-cask (or maintain a tap under CoolProp/homebrew-coolprop) so users can install via `brew install --cask coolprop`. Cask should pin the SHA256 of the .dmg per release.
acceptance_criteria: brew install --cask coolprop installs the latest release on a clean macOS; updates flow via brew upgrade.

title: GUI testing: cold-start and core-flow performance benchmarks
description: Measure (a) cold start time from process spawn to interactive window, (b) saturation-table compute time for a 100-pt R134a table, (c) iso-RH chart background generation time, (d) iso-line generation through a humid-air state point. Establish baseline thresholds and gate PRs that regress them by >20%.
acceptance_criteria: perf benchmark suite committed; CI runs it; thresholds in a config file; regression > threshold fails CI.

title: GUI testing: visual regression diffs in CI
description: Use the existing CI launch-screenshot infrastructure as a baseline: capture canonical screenshots for each tab (Calculator, Saturation, Humid Air), commit them under wrappers/GUI/tests/baselines/, and diff PR screenshots against the baseline using pixelmatch or odiff. Flag PRs that materially change rendering. Threshold tuned to ignore antialiasing jitter across runner OS upgrades.
acceptance_criteria: PR-side diff job runs, posts a summary comment when the diff exceeds threshold, and links to the diff image in the run artifacts.

title: Add get_last_pt_flash_tier instrumentation
description: dev/bench_ch4_h2s_grid.cpp references a get_last_pt_flash_tier() helper that does not exist in the codebase. Discovered during baseline work (CoolProp-aor): the bench was written against an instrumented build that was never committed. Add a thread-local counter in PT_flash that records which tier (Wilson-SS=1, GDEM=2, HELD=3) resolved the flash, and expose via a free function. Backfill into the new bench dev/bench_density_baseline.cpp so we can correlate timing tail with fallback tier.
acceptance_criteria: get_last_pt_flash_tier() returns 1/2/3 after a successful PT flash, 0 on error. Used by both bench_ch4_h2s_grid.cpp and bench_density_baseline.cpp.

title: Tier 3.4 — IWYU report job (warning-only artifact)
description:
include-what-you-use is already wired into CMakeLists.txt as an opt-in CMake variable. Add a CI job that runs an IWYU build and uploads the report as a downloadable artifact. Warning-only — IWYU output requires per-file judgment, so we never fail PRs on it.

Action:
- New .github/workflows/dev_iwyu.yml; apt install include-what-you-use; configure CMake with the IWYU opt-in plus -DCMAKE_EXPORT_COMPILE_COMMANDS=ON; build; capture output
- Trigger on PR + master push, weekly schedule fallback
- Always upload the IWYU log as artifact (if: always()); continue-on-error

Acceptance: IWYU report attached to every PR run as a downloadable artifact; job never blocks the PR.

title: Tier 3.3 — Coverity trigger review and document
description:
.github/workflows/dev_coverity.yml runs on dev_checks + 2x weekly schedule. Coverity Scan free tier has weekly run-rate limits (large projects: 21/week), so PR-triggered may not be feasible. Decide intent and bake it into the workflow.

Action:
- Confirm CoolProp project size category and Coverity quota
- Either: (a) keep scheduled-only and add a top-of-file comment in the YAML explaining why; or (b) switch to PR-triggered with a manual gating label (run-coverity)
- Update deprecated actions/checkout@v3 → v4 either way

Acceptance: trigger model is intentional and documented in the YAML, not leftover state.

value: Chebyshev mixture density rootfinder — CORRECT architecture (per Ian, 2026-04-25): precompute Chebyshev expansions of each individual EOS term in δ ONLY (not τ). Generalized-exponential terms factor cleanly into (τ-prefactor scalar) × (δ-function); the δ-function is static per-term and gets one Chebyshev expansion at compile/load time. At runtime evaluate the τ-prefactor closed-form, scale the per-term δ-expansion, sum across terms with corresponding-states weights to get α^r(τ_eval, δ) as a Chebyshev-in-δ polynomial. Rootfind in δ on that polynomial. ZERO build cost at runtime, ZERO composition dependence in precomputation. Memory: O(N_fluids + N_binary_pairs). Non-analytic and SAFT-associating terms don't factor and fall back to direct evaluation. This is the right framing for CoolProp-3gt — DO NOT propose per-z 2D builds or per-call 1D builds; those were both wrong.

Mixture density rootfinder baseline (CoolProp-aor): HelmholtzEOSMixtureBackend::solver_rho_Tp(T, p, rho_guess=-1) fails on 25-65% of (T,p) cells where the full PT flash succeeds. Tested on CH4/H2S 70/30 (65% fail), CO2/N2 80/20 (49% fail), n-Decane/Methane 30/70 (25% fail). Failures cluster near phase boundaries — Householder4 starting from the SRK guess hits inflection points and bails. The full PT flash succeeds because update_TP() funnels into solver_rho_Tp_global with bracketed Brent. So 'solver_rho_Tp by itself' has a huge fragility gap relative to 'orchestrated by the flash.' Implication: a Chebyshev or Gernert replacement must either (a) be robust as a standalone rootfinder or (b) provide a similar bracket/dispatch wrapper. CSV+PNG artifacts in dev/bench_density_*.

CoolProp PT flash p95 times (CoolProp-aor baseline, 60x60 grids): CH4/H2S 9 ms, CO2/N2 44 ms (worst, near-critical band), n-Decane/Methane 8 ms. Max single-cell flash time 414 ms on CO2/N2 in near-critical region. Solver_rho_Tp isolated p95: 33-194 μs, max ~1 ms. So full flash dominates by ~100x, but inside the flash solver_rho_Tp is called multiple times per phase + per Newton iter, so cumulative cost is closer.

When R1336mzz(Z) or any pure-fluid EOS changes, DO NOT manually edit source_eos_hash. Use fitcheb CLI at /Users/ianbell/Code/fastchebpure/build/fitcheb. Workflow per Ian 2026-04-25: (1) cd to fastchebpure/build, run: ./fitcheb -f FLUID -d COOLPROP_CHECKOUT (does fit+check). (2) Inject: ./fitcheb inject -f FLUID -d COOLPROP_CHECKOUT — replaces SUPERANCILLARY, adds check_points, stamps source_eos_hash. The older /Users/ianbell/Documents/Code/fastchebpure binary lacks the inject subcommand; ALWAYS use /Users/ianbell/Code/fastchebpure/build copy.

Mixture density baseline (CoolProp-aor) — full results across 4 systems, 60x60 PT grids:

Solver-fail rate of solver_rho_Tp(T,p,-1) where the FULL FLASH succeeds:
  amarillo (10-comp AGA-8):  1399/3586 (39%)  — flash p95 155 ms, solver max 5.2 ms
  CH4/H2S 70/30:             2214/3418 (65%)  — flash p95   9 ms, solver max 0.4 ms
  CO2/N2 80/20:              1740/3585 (49%)  — flash p95  44 ms, solver max 0.9 ms
  n-Decane/Methane 30/70:     895/3552 (25%)  — flash p95   8 ms, solver max 0.3 ms

Solver_rho_Tp itself scales badly with N components (5 ms max for 10-comp amarillo, vs 0.3 ms for binary c10c1). Failure regions cluster along the phase envelope. Replacement methods (Gernert, Chebyshev) must be evaluated on both isolation-robustness and N-scaling, not just steady-state cost.
